steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Both terms in the original function share a factor of $(2)^x$:
$$f(x) = 20(2)^x + 6(2)^x = (20 + 6)(2)^x = 26(2)^x$$

steps.title: Evaluate at the y-intercept
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs when $x = 0$:
$$f(0) = 26(2)^0 = 26(1) = 26$$
The y-coordinate of the y-intercept is 26. In the simplified form $f(x) = 26(2)^x$, this value appears directly as the coefficient. This matches choice D — D is correct.
